description_long: This object consists of three cylindrical medicine containers, two of which are white and one brown, all with their ends opened or broken, spilling out a granular, pill-like substance. The containers appear to be made of a combination of cardboard or paperboard and a white coating, possibly plastic or wax, with visible fibrous and granular textures at the broken ends. The spilled contents are off-white and irregularly shaped, resembling loose tablets or granules. The tops of the white containers have a circular marking, possibly an imprint or a seal, while the brown container has a more uniform, smooth exterior. The interiors of the containers are visible, showing a layered structure and the packed medicine inside. The overall appearance suggests discarded or damaged medicine packaging, with the contents partially exposed and scattered.
description: Three cylindrical medicine containers, two white and one brown, are broken open and spilling granular, pill-like contents. The packaging is fibrous and coated, with visible medicine granules scattered around.
description_view_0: Front view shows the open ends of all three containers, with the granular contents spilling out and the fibrous interior structure visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the tops of the white containers, showing circular markings and the scattered granules behind them.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the open backs of the containers and a large pile of spilled granules, emphasizing the hollow interiors.
description_view_3: This angle shows the side profiles of the containers, with the brown container's smooth exterior and the white containers' broken, fibrous ends.
